behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is algorithm quality inside the route-heuristic logic. No OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependency is required to expose a true regression in approximation ratio.
behaviors.test_description: Run deterministic fixture sets of coordinates (including adversarial layouts) with precomputed optimal tour lengths, assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case up to 20 stops. Include edge cases (collinear points, clusters, near-duplicates, and random seeded sets).

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often in wiring: distance outputs, road-type mapping, and speed configuration interaction. A single-function unit test can miss config key mismatches and module-boundary conversion bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process integration test that wires distance segment data, road-type tags, and speed configuration as production code does; verify per-leg and total ETA windows match expected formulas (time = distance/speed) including unit conversion and rounding rules.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is pure arithmetic/business rule evaluation (compare total estimated duration to remaining shift and compute excess). No I/O boundary is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Test validator function/object directly with table-driven cases: below limit, equal limit, above limit, and boundary precision cases. Assert rejection flag and exact excess duration returned for over-limit routes.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses a real external API boundary. In-process mocks can hide real contract and transport failures (response shape, status handling, empty-result semantics), which are critical to skip/report correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service test against a real geocoding HTTP API (or provider sandbox) with one intentionally unresolvable address and multiple valid ones. Assert output route excludes failed address, optimization still runs for remaining stops, and skipped-address report contains the failed input exactly once.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module behavior: input normalization/deduplication, item aggregation, and optimizer consumption. Unit tests of only dedupe logic may miss duplicate visitation caused by pipeline integration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use integration test through the route-planning pipeline with repeated addresses carrying different delivery items. Assert a single stop appears in planned route, aggregated item payload is preserved, and stop count/visit sequence contains no duplicate visit for that address.
